meta.title: Skills & Stack
meta.description: A grounded view of the tools I reach for — not a logo parade, but a map of real decisions made in real codebases.

bridge.description: A grounded view of what I reach for and where it's been deployed — not a logo parade, but a map of real decisions made in real codebases.

certification.label: Certification
certification.title: Issued by Google. Applied in production.
certification.description: Validated through hands-on work across Compute Engine, Cloud Storage, IAM policies, and CI/CD pipelines running on GCP.

grid.label: Full Stack Breakdown
grid.title: Every layer, mapped out.
grid.description: Select a category. Skills are grouped from exploratory to battle-tested.

foundations.label: // foundations
foundations.description: The medium, not the tools — present in every line of work

frontend.label: Frontend
frontend.annotation: Where precision meets perception — the layer users actually feel.

backend.label: Backend
backend.annotation: Mostly self-taught beyond the basics. Enough to ship real services and know when to stop and ask.

cloud.label: Cloud & DevOps
cloud.annotation: Certified on GCP, deployed on AWS. I treat infrastructure like code — version it, document it, don't improvise it.

tools.label: Tools & Practices
tools.annotation: The habits that make work trustworthy. Not glamorous, but the difference between a codebase you inherit and one you dread.

character.label: Character
character.title: How I actually show up.
character.description: Not a list of soft skills. A few honest observations about how I think, work, and relate to people — the kind of things you'd only know after a month working together.

statements.clarity: Complex problems don't stress me. Unclear problems do — so I make them clear first.
statements.directions: I think in multiple directions before I commit to one. It takes a moment. The moment is worth it.
statements.tangents: I digress. I explore tangents. I come back with something the straight line would have missed.
statements.slowDown: I'd rather slow down for clarity upfront than move fast toward the wrong thing.
statements.overengineering: Over-engineering is its own kind of dishonesty — it signals complexity where there isn't any.
statements.feedback: Feedback on my work isn't uncomfortable. It's the fastest way I know to get better.
statements.curious: I get genuinely curious about the person behind the requirements, not just the requirements.
statements.understand: I don't just ship code. I make sure the person next to me understands why it works the way it does.
statements.calm: I stay calm when things get intense. Not because I'm indifferent, but because someone has to.
